justifications.clarity: Five reference tables (quantization levels, hardware requirements, finance benchmarks, server selection, on-premise cost) plus code listings make a dense topic highly navigable; context and deepdive boxes provide motivation and technical depth at appropriate points.
justifications.rigor: LoRA equation (Eq. C.1) dimensionally verified; memory requirement corrected from 56 GB to 112 GB after math-checker advisory; six citations (araci2019finbert, wu2023bloomberggpt, hu2022lora, dettmers2023qlora, kwon2023vllm, llamacpp2023) cover all key empirical claims; benchmark scores marked as approximate.
justifications.completeness: Covers the full stack from Hub discovery through Transformers/Datasets API, finance model families, LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning with code, GGUF/llama.cpp, Ollama, TGI, vLLM with PagedAttention, on-premise cost analysis, compliance considerations, and a finance use case with worked Python example.
justifications.pedagogy: Seven measurable learning objectives stated upfront; worked Example C.1 provides a complete FinBERT sentiment factor pipeline from raw CSV to factor CSV; recommended patterns section gives six actionable, finance-specific operational guidelines.
justifications.style: Consistent with book LaTeX conventions throughout; British English spelling; booktabs tables; context and deepdive environments used appropriately; no inline paragraph labels.
